stemHtml: A student pilot wants to practise an RNP APCH to LPV minima. In the planning phase, the pilot finds out he/she needs a FAS data block (FAS DB). What is the FAS data block used for? It is used to:

options.a: Select a minimum descent altitude depending on the outside air temperature.
options.b: Programme the arrival route through waypoints and path terminators.
options.c: Feed the GNSS receiver with the differential corrections for the area.
options.d: Describe the final approach path, both laterally and vertically.
